sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Topics 86:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Topics 86:1
items.html: Because phase four did not leave a record. It is known that phase four serves as anterior to the vessel of Keter, which is the coarsest in the screen, called anterior. That which is not so coarse is considered the posterior of Keter. It is considered that its anterior is downward, and equalizes with the anterior of Hochma since it has become close to phase three of Hochma. Even though a record of phase four of clothing remains there, it is not enough to return the anterior of the vessel of Keter as in the beginning. Also, this causes the light of Keter to remain in the Peh, and not return in the second expansion. (Inner Light, Item‎ 18)
